stem: Evaluate \( \lim_{x \to 0^{+}} (1 + ([[a]])x)^{[[b]]/x} \), an indeterminate form of type \( 1^{\infty} \).
distractor_rationales: This adds the constants in the exponent instead of multiplying them.
This keeps only \( a \) in the exponent.
This keeps only \( b \) in the exponent.
This stops at \( \ln L = ab \) and forgets to exponentiate.
This treats the base as exactly 1, giving 1.
This has the wrong sign in the exponent.

stem: Evaluate \( \lim_{x \to 0^{+}} x^{[[a]]/\ln x} \), an indeterminate form of type \( 0^{0} \) (the base \( x \to 0 \) and the exponent \( \tfrac{[[a]]}{\ln x} \to 0 \)).
solution: Let \( L \) be the limit. Then \( \ln L = \lim_{x \to 0^{+}} \frac{[[a]]}{\ln x}\cdot \ln x = [[a]] \), since the \( \ln x \) factors cancel exactly (they never actually reach their individual limits of \( \pm\infty \) and \( 0 \) — the log transform reveals the true, finite exponent). Therefore \( L = e^{[[a]]} \).
distractor_rationales: This wrongly assumes a \( 0^0 \) form always evaluates to 0.
This wrongly assumes a \( 0^0 \) form always evaluates to 1.
This has the wrong sign in the exponent.
This stops at \( \ln L = a \) and forgets to exponentiate.
This doubles the correct exponent.
This is off by one from the correct limit.

stem: Evaluate \( \lim_{x \to \infty} (x^{2} + 1)^{[[a]]/\ln x} \), an indeterminate form of type \( \infty^{0} \) (the base \( \to \infty \) and the exponent \( \tfrac{[[a]]}{\ln x} \to 0 \)).
solution: Let \( L \) be the limit. Then \( \ln L = \lim_{x \to \infty} \frac{[[a]]}{\ln x}\cdot \ln(x^{2}+1) \). Since \( \ln(x^{2}+1) \sim 2\ln x \) for large \( x \), this gives \( \ln L = [[a]]\cdot 2 = 2([[a]]) \). Therefore \( L = e^{2([[a]])} \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets the factor of 2 coming from \( \ln(x^2+1)\sim 2\ln x \).
This wrongly assumes an \( \infty^0 \) form always evaluates to 1.
This has the wrong sign as well as the factor of 2.
This stops at \( \ln L = 2a \) worth of reasoning but forgets to exponentiate, reporting just \( a \).
This uses a factor of 3 instead of the correct factor of 2.
This is off by one from the correct limit.

stem: Evaluate \( \lim_{x \to \infty} \left(1 + \dfrac{[[a]]}{x}\right)^{[[b]]x} \), an indeterminate form of type \( 1^{\infty} \).
solution: Let \( L \) be the limit. Then \( \ln L = \lim_{x \to \infty} [[b]]x \ln\left(1 + \dfrac{[[a]]}{x}\right) = \lim_{x \to \infty} [[b]]x\left(\dfrac{[[a]]}{x} + o\!\left(\dfrac{1}{x}\right)\right) = ([[a]])([[b]]) \). Therefore \( L = e^{([[a]])([[b]])} \).
distractor_rationales: This adds the constants in the exponent instead of multiplying them.
This keeps only \( a \) in the exponent.
This keeps only \( b \) in the exponent.
This stops at \( \ln L = ab \) and forgets to exponentiate.
This treats the base as exactly 1, giving 1.
This has the wrong sign in the exponent.
